rir_experience_bias.note: RIR underprediction scales inversely with training experience (months)

rir_rep_penalty.source: Zourdos et al. 2016; Hackett et al. 2012
rir_rep_penalty.note: RIR accuracy degrades on high-rep sets

rir_distance_penalty.note: reports 3+ from failure are systematically further than stated

proximity_target_rir.source: Refalo et al. 2024 (hypertrophy); Robinson et al. 2024 meta-regression, 67 studies (strength)
proximity_target_rir.note: productive proximity-to-failure window per goal; midpoint is the target RIR

rep_ranges.note: effective rep ranges by goal and movement type

readiness_signal_weights.note: relative weights of the five base readiness signals; sum to 1

readiness_checkin_signal.note: daily check-in S6 weight (asymmetric positive cap) and its sleep/soreness/energy sub-weights

readiness_thresholds.note: composite-readiness band boundaries for the decision table

expected_weekly_gain.source: Gonzalez-Badillo et al.; training-age progression norms
expected_weekly_gain.note: expected weekly e1RM gain as a fraction, by training age

deload_triggers.note: deload = ~10% back-off; force a deload week after this many sessions without one

fatigue_masking.note: flat e1RM + elevated ACWR + rising RPE = masked fitness → cut volume, not load

increase_vetoes.note: block increases with <3 sessions logged, or >14 days off

safety_caps.note: never exceed 105% of best historical e1RM; cap a single jump at min(10%, 10 lb)

weekly_e1rm_increase_cap.note: max weekly e1RM increase; novices adapt faster than experienced lifters

ml_blend.note: alpha = min(alpha_max, sessions/sessions_denom); how fast ML earns trust

volume_counting.note: secondary muscles get a fraction of a set; hard set = within target RIR + margin

volume_landmarks_default.note: sets per muscle per week; large individual variation

volume_landmarks_overrides.note: fast-recovering small muscles tolerate more; lower back less

volume_calibration_alpha.note: how fast a user's MEV/MAV/MRV estimates move toward observed tolerance
